key_insight: The paper establishes the first convergence theory for fully discrete least-squares splitting schemes for the 2D Monge-Ampère equation using finite elements, showing that the iterative method converges locally and its limit achieves optimal-order H2-type accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This provides a rigorous FEM foundation for solving fully nonlinear PDEs in computational mechanics and geometry, with practical support for C1, C0-interior penalty, and DG discretizations.
method: Reconstruct the Hessian with finite elements and enforce the determinant constraint through a local projection step.
Combine the splitting iteration with a discrete Miranda-Talenti estimate to prove local convergence.
Analyze convergence for Argyris, C0-interior penalty, and DG schemes, including lower-order cases under smallness assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports proven optimal-order convergence and numerical experiments that confirm the theoretical rates across the supported discretizations.
watch_item: The convergence guarantees are local and some quadratic C0-interior penalty and DG cases require sufficiently small H3 seminorms of the exact solution.
paper_title: Fully discrete least-squares splitting scheme for the Monge-Ampère equation: finite element analysis and convergence
